description: Enable Codex (codex-cli 0.135.0+) as a third runner provider alongside Claude and Grok, reachable only via an explicit primaryRunner spec with provider: "codex". Carries provider intent from config to spawn site (no model-string inference), adds a CodexRunner + stream parser, preserves provider identity through recovery, validates+probes the Codex binary, and guards orchestrator state against Codex sandbox writes. v1 scope per the architect-reviewed plan at /home/chris/.claude/plans/plan-out-th-necessary-nifty-storm.md.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` output
Rust: Scoped tests pass with `cargo test -p task-mgr <module>`
No breaking changes to Claude/Grok behavior — a run with no Codex config must be byte-identical to HEAD (gate every Codex-specific branch on the provider/runner being Codex)
No literal model-string creep outside src/loop_engine/model.rs (tests/no_hardcoded_models.rs guards this)
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio); we are pre-launch, foundations compound
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — qualityDimensions on every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Phase 2 foundation — prefer solutions that lay strong post-launch foundations (1:10+ savings ratio)
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at REVIEW-001 — REVIEW must leave the trunk green including pre-existing failures
Ship working code with tests to prove it; handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)
Abstractions with only one concrete use
Error messages that don't identify what went wrong
Catch-all error handlers that swallow context
Wildcard match arms (_ =>) on RunnerKind / Provider / CrashType — exhaustive matching is the safety property that forces every new provider to be handled at every dispatch site

Re-deriving the runner inside recovery/overflow from the model string instead of threading the executed RunnerKind — the model string for a Codex task is gpt-* and re-derives to Claude (silent cross-provider escape)
Inferring Codex from gpt-*/o*/codex model strings — v1 routes to Codex ONLY via an explicit primaryRunner provider hint

requires.reason: models-routing-config lands the provider/model routing CLI + config setters that touch the same surfaces codex-runner extends (src/loop_engine/model.rs, src/loop_engine/project_config.rs, src/commands/models/). Sequencing it first avoids merge conflicts and lets Codex routing build on the settled config surface.

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: resolve_task_execution_target sets provider_hint=Some(Codex) ONLY when a primaryRunner spec with provider:"codex" matches by task type or id prefix
Positive: resolve_effective_runner resolution order is runner_overrides -> provider_hint -> provider_for_model -> Claude
Negative: provider_for_model(Some("gpt-5")) == Provider::Claude AND provider_for_model(Some("codex")) == Provider::Claude AND provider_for_model(Some("o3")) == Provider::Claude
Negative: explicit tasks.model present -> resolve_task_execution_target returns that model with provider_hint=None even if a primaryRunner spec would otherwise match
Unit test: parse_config_provider("  CODEX ") == Ok(Provider::Codex); parse_config_provider("openai") is Err
Unit test: a Codex-routed SlotResult survives process_slot_result without panic (provider_hint threaded into the slot.rs:570 assertion)

Known-bad: a re-derivation at slot.rs:570 that passes only the model string (no hint) would assert_eq!(Claude, Codex) and panic — test must route a Codex slot through process_slot_result and assert no panic
Grep-test: no production (non-#[cfg(test)]) code calls the bare-Option From form of resolve_effective_runner
CONTRACT: parse_config_provider accepts exactly the provider strings RunnerSpec.provider can hold (grep RunnerSpec usage to confirm claude|grok|codex coverage)
userStories.description:
Carry provider intent from the primaryRunner config spec to the spawn site instead of re-inferring it from the model string. This is the foundation every later task depends on.

DO:
- Add `Codex` to the `Provider` enum (model.rs:53). Handle the new variant at EVERY match (compile errors are the guard).
- Add `parse_config_provider(s: &str) -> Result<Provider, String>` — the ONLY place a config provider string becomes a Provider (claude|grok|codex, trimmed, case-insensitive; else Err).
- Add `ResolvedExecutionTarget { model: Option<String>, provider_hint: Option<Provider> }` and `resolve_task_execution_target(ctx) -> ResolvedExecutionTarget`. Mirror resolve_task_model's rung order, but set `provider_hint` ONLY on the rung-2 primary_runner_match branch (parsed via parse_config_provider). Every other rung yields provider_hint: None.
- Reduce `resolve_task_model` to a thin wrapper returning `.model` so existing callers compile unchanged.
- Change `resolve_effective_runner` (engine.rs:368) to take an explicit `EffectiveRunnerInput { model, provider_hint }`. New order: runner_overrides -> provider_hint -> provider_for_model(model) -> Claude.
- Restrict the `From<Option<&str>>` ergonomic conversion (provider_hint=None) to `#[cfg(test)]`.
- Update ALL call sites to pass explicit input: sequential spawn (iteration.rs:446), wave pre-spawn (wave_scheduler.rs:1162), and the merge re-derive assertion (slot.rs:570). For slot.rs:570, add `provider_hint: Option<Provider>` to SlotResult/SlotContext, populate it on the main thread at spawn, and rebuild EffectiveRunnerInput here so the re-derivation is byte-identical to spawn-time.

DO NOT:
- Change `provider_for_model` (model.rs:83) — it must keep returning only Claude/Grok. A codex/gpt-*/o* model string must NOT classify as Codex.
- Leave any production call of the bare-Option `resolve_effective_runner` form.

Edge cases:
- gpt-*/o*/codex model string with no primaryRunner hint -> resolves to Claude (not Codex).
- primaryRunner match + explicit tasks.model -> tasks.model wins (rung 1), provider_hint absent.
- A Codex-routed wave slot reaching slot.rs:570 -> assertion passes (no panic).

Known-bad to guard against:
- Threading only model and re-deriving the runner from it -> Codex slot (gpt-* model) mis-asserts as Claude and panics the wave worker (release assert_eq!, Learning 3110).
edgeCases: gpt-*/o*/codex model string, no primaryRunner hint: routes to Claude
primaryRunner match AND explicit tasks.model: tasks.model wins, no hint
primaryRunner match by both byTaskType and byIdPrefix: byTaskType wins (existing precedence)
Codex-routed wave slot at slot.rs:570 merge assertion: passes, no panic

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Compile error preferred (explicit EffectiveRunnerInput); test-only From shortcut means production cannot accidentally pass None

failureModes.cause: Codex slot reaches merge assertion with no hint
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Hint threaded via SlotResult so re-derivation matches spawn-time runner; no panic

userStories.notes: Reuse: model.rs primary_runner_match (rung-2 logic), ModelResolutionContext (model.rs:235 carries primary_runner). engine.rs:374 has the From<Option<&str>> conversion to restrict. Learning 3547/3733/3070: RunnerKind dispatch + provider_for_model fallback pattern — keep exhaustive matching. Learning 3110: drift sentinels at engine/slot are release assert! (NOT debug_assert!), so a hint mismatch is a production panic — this is exactly why the slot.rs:570 re-derivation must receive the hint. Data contract: RunnerSpec{provider:String, model:String} (project_config.rs:55) -> parse_config_provider -> Provider -> RunnerKind.
qualityDimensions: Exhaustive matching on Provider/RunnerKind — no wildcard arms
provider_hint set on exactly one rung (primary_runner_match); None everywhere else
resolve_task_model remains a behavior-preserving wrapper (existing callers untouched)
Hint cannot be silently dropped: production sites pass explicit EffectiveRunnerInput, From shortcut is test-only
Pure-Claude/Grok routing byte-identical to HEAD
userStories.title: Provider-intent threading: Provider::Codex + ResolvedExecutionTarget + explicit EffectiveRunnerInput

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: dispatch(RunnerKind::Codex, ...) spawns the Codex binary with the exact argv for the given PermissionMode (Scoped/Auto vs Dangerous shapes verified)
Positive: the prompt is delivered on stdin and stdin is closed; output is read only from accumulated agent_message
Positive: CodexRunner.supports() returns true only for StreamJson; false for Effort/Pty/DisallowedTools/TitleArtifactCleanup
Negative: enforce_capabilities rejects RunnerOpts with effort/use_pty/disallowed_tools/cleanup_title_artifact set for RunnerKind::Codex BEFORE spawn (UnsupportedRunnerCapability)
Negative: turn.completed lines contribute nothing to derive_output
Unit test (stream): normal (agent_message), tool (command_execution), type:"error", turn.failed, malformed line, split completion tag, tail cap — each parsed/accumulated as specified
Unit test (dispatch mock): a fake $CODEX_BINARY echoing canned JSONL is invoked (not Claude) for RunnerKind::Codex; argv order, stdin prompt, cwd, and -m flag presence/absence asserted
Known-bad: a mock that emits plain text instead of JSONL would silently yield empty output — the mock MUST emit valid Codex JSONL (Learning 3853)
Full RunnerKind x RunnerCapability matrix test includes all four Codex negative capabilities
CONTRACT: CodexStreamFormat StreamEvent variants match the StreamEvent enum in stream.rs (grep to verify)
userStories.description:
Add the Codex runner: a new RunnerKind variant, a CodexRunner implementing LlmRunner, exhaustive dispatch wiring, the capability matrix, and a CodexStreamFormat JSONL parser.

DO:
- Add `Codex` to RunnerKind (runner.rs:226). Fill the supports (:238) and dispatch (:1513) matches exhaustively — no wildcard arms.
- Add `pub(crate) struct CodexRunner;` impl LlmRunner (runner.rs:298), modelled on GrokRunner (runner.rs:730) but written fresh (see Learning 2889 — do NOT copy-paste argv).
- supports(): StreamJson => true; Effort, Pty, DisallowedTools, TitleArtifactCleanup => false.
- Binary resolution: $CODEX_BINARY if nonempty/trimmed, else `codex` on PATH (mirror resolve_and_verify_grok_binary, project_config.rs:376).
- Prompt via STDIN (codex ... exec ... -), not argv, not --prompt-file. Write the full prompt on a dedicated writer thread (or handle BrokenPipe) so an early Codex exit (auth failure) doesn't deadlock a blocking write_all to a full pipe.
- argv (permission-mode dependent): Scoped/Auto -> `codex -a never exec --json --sandbox workspace-write --ephemeral --skip-git-repo-check --cd <cwd> [-m <model>] -`; Dangerous -> `codex exec --json --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox --ephemeral --skip-git-repo-check --cd <cwd> [-m <model>] -`.
- No session-id / no cleanup_session (Codex --ephemeral leaves no artifact; the dispatch cleanup branch only runs when session_id.is_some()).
- Add `CodexStreamFormat` (stream.rs:70 trait, model on GrokStreamFormat stream.rs:371): item.completed agent_message -> AssistantText (sole authoritative output); command_execution start/complete (+aggregated_output) -> ToolUse/ToolResult (display only); type:"error"/turn.failed -> Error; thread.started/turn.started -> no-op; turn.completed -> IGNORE for output (usage only). derive_output = tail-capped accumulated agent_message (reuse tail_truncate / MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES). Set line_buffer_tee per observed granularity.

DO NOT:
- Use turn.completed as output (drops <task-status>/<completed> lifecycle tags that ride inside agent_message).
- Copy GrokRunner's --prompt-file/argv prompt delivery (Codex uses stdin).
- Add a wildcard dispatch arm.

Edge cases:
- Malformed JSONL line: skipped, parsing continues.
- <task-status>/<completed> tag split across two agent_message chunks: extracted by shared drive_stream accumulation (no special handling).
- type:"error" with invalid model: surfaced as Error, exit 1.

Known-bad to guard against:
- Reusing GrokRunner argv construction wholesale -> flag-ordering bug (Learning 2889: a double -p bug shipped during Grok work even with the reference open).
edgeCases: Malformed JSONL line: skip, continue parsing
Completion tag split across agent_message chunks: extracted by shared accumulation
type:"error" (invalid model): surfaced as Error event, exit 1
Codex exits before stdin drained: writer thread/BrokenPipe avoids deadlock

failureModes.cause: Blocking write_all to a full stdin pipe on a dead Codex child
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Dedicated writer thread or BrokenPipe handling prevents deadlock

userStories.notes: Reuse: GrokRunner (runner.rs:730) as a STRUCTURAL reference only; LlmRunner trait (runner.rs:298); GrokStreamFormat (stream.rs:371); drive_stream (stream.rs:247); tail_truncate/MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES; CHECKS (runner.rs:1425) + enforce_capabilities (runner.rs:1460). Test helper: make_stream_json_result_script pattern (Learning 673) for mock JSONL. Learning 2889: write argv fresh, do NOT copy Grok's — flag ordering bugs are the #1 second-runner failure. Learning 3068: .expect() is acceptable for guaranteed IO invariants in runner.rs IF documented why it's safe. Learning 3220: provider-specific cleanup via trait method (Codex needs none — ephemeral).
qualityDimensions: Exhaustive RunnerKind dispatch — no wildcard arm
argv written fresh per Codex CLI surface, not copied from Grok
stdin prompt delivery is deadlock-safe (writer thread / BrokenPipe handled)
Output sourced ONLY from accumulated agent_message; turn.completed ignored
Capability matrix rejects unshaped RunnerOpts before spawn
userStories.title: CodexRunner + RunnerKind::Codex dispatch + CodexStreamFormat

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: handle_task_failure and escalate_task_model_if_needed_inner accept executed_runner and gate escalation/promotion on it != Codex
Positive: handle_overflow returns RecoveryAction::Blocked immediately for effective_runner == Codex, before any rung
Positive: CodexAuthFailure is non-counting via a shared helper used by BOTH sequential and wave paths
Negative: a Codex RuntimeError (NULL DB model) writes NO model to tasks.model and triggers NO Grok promotion (assert tasks.model unchanged)
Negative: a Codex PromptTooLong writes NO Claude model to tasks.model
Unit/body-grep test: escalate_task_model_if_needed_inner does NOT call resolve_effective_runner (mirror runtime_error_fallback.rs grep-test pattern)
Unit test (sequential + wave): Codex crash increments consecutive_failures only as intended; no wave assertion panic
Negative-control test: a task whose agent_message contains "HTTP 401" is NOT classified as CodexAuthFailure (structured signal required) so it still auto-blocks after max retries
CONTRACT: IterationResult.effective_runner is populated at the spawn site for ALL runners (grep the iteration/wave spawn paths to verify it is always set, not Option-defaulted)
userStories.description:
Stop a Codex crash from being treated as a Claude/Grok failure. Thread the EXECUTED runner out of the iteration and into recovery/overflow so escalation and promotion never fire for Codex.

DO:
- Add `effective_runner: RunnerKind` to IterationResult (engine.rs:175). SlotResult already has effective_runner (engine.rs:463); also carry the provider_hint added in FEAT-001 if needed by recovery.
- Add `executed_runner: RunnerKind` params to handle_task_failure (recovery.rs:378) and escalate_task_model_if_needed_inner (recovery.rs:153), sourced from IterationResult.effective_runner at the sequential call site (orchestrator.rs:1335) and the wave call site (wave_scheduler.rs ~1386).
- FORBID any internal resolve_effective_runner call inside recovery — gate escalation/Grok-promotion branches on `executed_runner != RunnerKind::Codex`.
- Add `CrashType::CodexAuthFailure` (config.rs:461) modelled on GrokAuthFailure, and `TaskMgrError::CodexAuthFailure { hint }` (error.rs) modelled on GrokAuthFailure. Route it through a SHARED auth-skip-list helper so sequential and wave both treat it as non-counting (does not increment consecutive_failures).
- Add explicit early short-circuit at the TOP of handle_overflow (reactions/post_output.rs:142): `if effective_runner == RunnerKind::Codex { return RecoveryAction::Blocked }` — before any rung walk. (select_fallback_target already returns None for Codex.)

DO NOT:
- Re-derive the runner from the model string anywhere in recovery/overflow (a Codex task's gpt-* model re-derives to Claude -> Opus escalation -> silent Claude route).
- Rely on rung-1/2/3 preconditions happening to be false for Codex — rung-2 escalate_model keys off the model string and could write a Claude model for a gpt-* string.
- Misclassify agent output mentioning "401" as an auth failure (must be a STRUCTURED signal: type:"error"+turn.failed, or stderr 401/missing-bearer).

Edge cases:
- Codex RuntimeError with NULL DB model: consecutive_failures increments, but NO Claude model write, NO Grok promotion.
- Codex PromptTooLong overflow: straight to Blocked, no model written to tasks.model.
- CodexAuthFailure (repeated 401s, exit 1 — NOT fast-fail like Grok): non-counting on both sequential and wave.
- Wave Codex crash: no assertion panic, no wave-wide model corruption.

Known-bad to guard against:
- A negative-control miss where a task whose agent OUTPUT discusses HTTP 401 gets classified non-counting -> it never auto-blocks and retries forever (Learning 3109).
edgeCases: Codex RuntimeError, NULL DB model: counts toward retries; no escalation/promotion
Codex PromptTooLong: Blocked, no model write
CodexAuthFailure (repeated 401s, exit 1, not fast-fail): non-counting on both paths
Wave Codex crash: no assertion panic, no wave-wide model corruption

failureModes.cause: Recovery re-derives runner from gpt-* model string
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Forbidden — executed_runner is threaded; Codex gate fires reliably
NOT classified as auth failure; only a structured type:error+turn.failed or stderr bearer signal counts

userStories.notes: Reuse: GrokAuthFailure handling (config.rs CrashType:461, error.rs, recovery skip-list), PendingPromotion (recovery.rs:49), select_fallback_target (post_output.rs:47 already returns None for Codex), handle_overflow 5-rung ladder (post_output.rs:142). Learning 3109: stderr-sniff/auth substring lists need a maintenance runbook + negative-control tests — add one. Learning 2852: wave PromptTooLong recovery uses the shared ladder (don't fork it). Learning 3110: the slot drift assertion is a release assert — recovery must not feed it a mismatched runner. Extract the auth-skip-list helper once; do not duplicate the skip condition across sequential and wave.
qualityDimensions: Executed runner is THREADED, never re-derived in recovery/overflow
Single shared auth-skip-list helper used by sequential and wave (DRY)
Codex overflow short-circuits to Blocked at the top, not by rung-precondition luck
Auth classification requires a structured signal, with a negative control
No Claude model string ever written to tasks.model for a Codex task
userStories.title: Preserve provider identity through recovery + overflow + non-counting Codex auth crash

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: config load rejects an unknown primaryRunner provider (e.g. "openai") and a blank provider/model with a clear error
Positive: config load rejects reviewModel set together with any Codex primaryRunner route
Positive: config load rejects enabled fallbackRunner with provider != "grok"
Positive: check_codex_primary_binary verifies the executable bit (mirrors runtime resolution exactly) and runs only when a Codex primary route exists
Negative: with no Codex primary route, the Codex binary is NOT probed (no PATH lookup)
Unit test: Codex binary probe for empty / whitespace / missing / non-executable / executable $CODEX_BINARY (5 cases)
Unit test: reviewModel + Codex primary route -> config load Err; reviewModel alone (no Codex route) -> Ok

Invariant test: no code path inserts RunnerKind::Codex into ctx.runner_overrides (Codex is a primary route only, never a fallback target — else the route-gated probe is skipped and an unverified binary spawns mid-run)
CONTRACT: resolve_and_verify_codex_binary uses the same $CODEX_BINARY -> PATH precedence and exec-bit check as CodexRunner's runtime resolution (grep both to confirm parity)
userStories.description:
Validate Codex-related config before the startup binary probe, and probe the Codex binary before the first iteration.

DO:
- Reject unknown/blank provider and blank model in every RunnerSpec under primaryRunner.byTaskType / byIdPrefix, using parse_config_provider (FEAT-001).
- Reject reviewModel combined with any Codex primaryRunner route (reviewModel is string-only in v1 and clears the provider hint -> an operator setting both would silently get a non-Codex review). Fail config load with a clear message.
- Reject enabled fallbackRunner.provider != "grok" (a provider:"codex" fallback would write a Codex model but still dispatch Grok at rung-4).
- Add `check_codex_primary_binary(primary: Option<&PrimaryRunnerConfig>)` mirroring check_fallback_runner_binary (project_config.rs:408) + `resolve_and_verify_codex_binary` mirroring resolve_and_verify_grok_binary (project_config.rs:376): $CODEX_BINARY -> `codex` on PATH, check the executable bit. Probe ONLY when an explicit Codex primary route exists. Call it from src/main.rs alongside the existing fallback/review probes, before iteration 1.

DO NOT:
- Probe the Codex binary when no Codex primary route is configured (no surprise PATH lookups).
- Accept a provider string the dispatcher can't route.

Edge cases:
- $CODEX_BINARY empty/whitespace: treated as unset, fall back to PATH.
- $CODEX_BINARY set to a non-executable / missing file: probe fails with a helpful NotFound error before iteration 1.
- primaryRunner present but no Codex spec: Codex probe skipped.

Known-bad to guard against:
- A probe that checks file existence but NOT the executable bit (Learning 3108: probe must mirror the runtime resolver AND check the exec bit).
edgeCases: $CODEX_BINARY empty/whitespace: treated as unset -> PATH
$CODEX_BINARY non-executable/missing: probe fails before iteration 1
primaryRunner present, no Codex spec: probe skipped

failureModes.cause: Codex binary missing at startup with a Codex route configured
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Loop exits before iteration 1 with a helpful NotFound error

failureModes.cause: Operator sets reviewModel + Codex primary route
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Config load fails with an explicit message (string reviewModel would silently win)

userStories.notes: Reuse: check_fallback_runner_binary (project_config.rs:408), check_review_model_binary (project_config.rs:430), resolve_and_verify_grok_binary (project_config.rs:376), is_executable_path. main.rs binary-probe call site (alongside fallback/review probes, before iteration 1). Learning 3108: startup probe must mirror the runtime resolver AND check the executable bit — paired invariant. Learning 3109: validation error messages should be actionable (name the bad field + allowed values).
qualityDimensions: Probe mirrors runtime resolution exactly (same precedence + exec-bit check)
Probe runs only when a Codex primary route exists (no surprise PATH lookups)
Config errors name the offending field and allowed values
reviewModel + Codex and non-Grok fallback are rejected at load, not at spawn
userStories.title: Config validation + Codex binary probe

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: text artifact (tasks/*.json, *-prompt.md, .last-branch) mutated by a simulated Codex run is detected and restored to the snapshot content
Positive: a simulated integrity-PRESERVING SQLite change (e.g. a valid UPDATE mimicking a task-mgr write) is ALLOWED — loop continues, no fatal
Positive: SQLite corruption (truncated/garbage db/wal/shm) is detected via integrity check -> fatal, loop stops, NO raw restore performed
Negative: with the executed runner being Claude or Grok, the guard is a no-op (byte-identical to HEAD — verified by an existing sequential/wave test staying green)
Unit test: regular->symlink swap is detected via inode change AND restore removes the symlink before writing (assert the link TARGET was not written through)
Unit test: hardlink/inode-swap on a protected file is detected
Unit test: a NEW file matching a protected glob is detected
Unit test (wave): the protected window EXCLUDES the orchestrator's own merge-back/reconcile writes (a normal wave does not trip the guard)
Unit test (wave): a Codex SQLite corruption fails the whole wave THROUGH the cascade-halt sentinel (consecutive_merge_fail_waves is NOT reset)
Negative: no post-output mutation (tag processing/overflow/merge) runs before the verify barrier (ordering test)
CONTRACT: protected text globs and the db_dir/tasks path resolution match how env.rs/db path.rs compute tasks_dir and the SQLite trio location (grep to verify)
userStories.description:
Guard orchestrator state against Codex sandbox writes. Codex can write inside --cd cwd and /tmp; the worktree cwd contains orchestrator state. This guard DETECTS AND REVERTS post-hoc — it does NOT prevent in-run corruption (state that explicitly).

DO:
- Create src/loop_engine/protected_state.rs and register it in src/loop_engine/mod.rs (absent at HEAD; DISCARD any untracked WIP file and build fresh).
- Text artifacts under db_dir/tasks (**/*.json, **/*-prompt.md, .last-branch): snapshot (content hash + (dev, inode)) before, verify after, RESTORE on mismatch. These are not written by task-mgr CLI during a task run, so exact-match restore is correct.
- SQLite files (tasks.db, tasks.db-wal, tasks.db-shm): INTEGRITY-based detection, NOT byte-hash. Codex (and other runners) MAY legitimately run `task-mgr` CLI commands that mutate the DB — those keep it valid. Detect CORRUPTION not CHANGE: PRAGMA quick_check/integrity_check passes, the DB still opens, schema/user_version has not regressed. On failure -> FATAL: suppress output processing, stop loop, NO raw restore. Integrity-preserving byte changes are allowed.
- Escape protection (full dev/inode tracking): no-follow handling (symlink_metadata + no-follow reads); record (dev, inode) per protected file; on verify, a path that changed inode (regular->symlink swap or hardlink target swap) is a mutation/escape. De-symlink before restore (remove_file a symlink, then write a regular file — never fs::write through an unverified path). Canonicalize-and-contain the parent before create_dir_all. Detect NEW files matching protected globs, not just mutations.
- Guard gate = positive allowlist: `fn runner_requires_state_guard(kind) -> bool` (true for Codex in v1). Claude/Grok return false -> byte-identical to today.
- Sequential ordering (iteration.rs / iteration_pipeline.rs): snapshot before spawn; verify BEFORE tag processing, overflow, retry tracking, or any post-output mutation. On fatal SQLite corruption: suppress output, stop loop.
- Wave ordering (wave_scheduler.rs): the verify barrier sits BETWEEN child completion and any orchestrator post-output work (before process_slot_result -> handle_overflow -> process_iteration_output -> merge_slot_branches_with_resolver/reconcile). Snapshot after all claims + pre-spawn writes, capturing state at that instant, so the orchestrator's own merge-back/reconcile/lifecycle writes are EXCLUDED from the protected window. On mutation: fail the whole wave, routed through the cascade-halt sentinel (apply_merge_fail_reset_and_halt_check semantics), NOT a bare early-return (which would zero consecutive_merge_fail_waves and defeat the synthetic-deadlock halt).

DO NOT:
- Byte-hash tasks.db (would trip on legitimate task-mgr CLI writes).
- Raw-restore the SQLite trio (assumption 11 — risks worse corruption).
- fs::write to a path you haven't re-verified is a regular file (restore would become an arbitrary-write primitive through an attacker symlink).
- Gate on a bare `== Codex` (use the allowlist helper).

Edge cases:
- Codex replaces tasks/foo.json (regular) with a symlink to an out-of-tree file: detected via inode change; restore removes the symlink then writes the regular file (does NOT write through it).
- Codex creates a NEW tasks/evil.json: detected as a new matching path.
- Codex runs `task-mgr complete X` (legitimate DB mutation): integrity passes -> ALLOWED, loop continues.
- Codex raw-corrupts tasks.db-wal: integrity fails -> fatal, loop stops, no restore.

Known-bad to guard against:
- A guard that compares tasks.db bytes -> every legitimate task-mgr CLI write trips it (false fatal). Detect integrity, not change.
- A restore that fs::write()s the snapshot through a path now pointing at a symlink -> arbitrary write to the link target.
edgeCases: regular->symlink swap: detected via inode; restore de-symlinks first
new matching file created by Codex: detected
legitimate task-mgr CLI DB write: integrity passes, allowed
raw SQLite corruption: integrity fails, fatal, no restore

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Integrity check fails -> fatal: suppress output, stop loop (sequential) / fail wave via cascade-halt (wave); no raw restore

failureModes.cause: Codex swaps a JSON file for a symlink to /home/chris/.bashrc
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Inode change detected; symlink removed; regular file restored; link target untouched
Must not — barrier sits before post-output work so legitimate orchestrator writes are excluded

userStories.notes: Reuse: env.rs path resolution (tasks_dir, LAST_BRANCH_FILE), db path resolution for the tasks.db trio, wave_scheduler merge-fail/cascade-halt helper (apply_merge_fail_reset_and_halt_check semantics), IterationResult.effective_runner (FEAT-003a) for the guard gate. Learning 1583: prior approach used path-scoped Edit/Write DENIALS in the agent permission config to stop loop-agent JSON mutation — that's a Claude-permission mechanism and does NOT cover Codex's own sandbox; this guard is the Codex-side complement (detect/revert), so document the distinction. SQLite detection = rusqlite PRAGMA quick_check + open + user_version, NOT a content hash. State in the module doc: TOCTOU between snapshot and verify is inherent; this detects/reverts, it does not prevent.
qualityDimensions: SQLite guard is integrity-based (permits legitimate task-mgr CLI writes); text guard is exact-match restore
Full dev/inode tracking closes symlink-swap and hardlink-escape; restore never writes through an unverified path
Positive runner allowlist gate (extensible), Claude/Grok no-op
Wave barrier excludes orchestrator's own writes; fatal routes through cascade-halt, not bare return
Module doc states detect-and-revert, not prevent (TOCTOU acknowledged)

If no issues: emit `<task-status>REVIEW-001:done</task-status>` with 'Clean review' note. Use the rust-python-code-reviewer agent for the security-sensitive protected_state.rs review. Decisions to record post-loop (per plan): provider intent carried as ResolvedExecutionTarget not inferred; Codex SQLite guard is integrity-based fatal-detect (no raw restore); guard is a positive runner allowlist that detects/reverts (not prevents). Verify the no_hardcoded_models.rs test still passes (no literal model strings outside model.rs).
